message:
fix(ft-api): tensor_block_diag autograd via apply_function with per-block extraction backward [bd-w6l2]

tensor_block_diag used to extract values from each input, build
the diagonal assembly in plain f64, and rebuild a
requires_grad=false leaf — same severed-autograd pattern recently
fixed for cross / diag_embed / matrix_norm.

Forward is purely linear in each input — output[row, col] is
block_k[r, c] when (row, col) is in block k's region, 0 otherwise.
Backward extracts each block from the corresponding region of
grad_output:
   grad_block_k[r, c] = grad_output[row_offset_k + r, col_offset_k + c]

Wrap in tensor_apply_function with a Vec<Option<Vec<f64>>>
backward that splits grad_output into per-input blocks. The
per-block (rows, cols, row_offset, col_offset) tuples are
pre-computed once and captured by both the forward and backward
closures so the coordinate mapping is consistent in both
directions.

Adds block_diag_propagates_gradient_through_each_block regression
test: two 2x2 blocks A=[[1,2],[3,4]], B=[[5,6],[7,8]] in a 4x4
output, weighted-sum loss against weights = 0..15 reshaped to 4x4.
Expected gradients are the corresponding 2x2 sub-matrices:
grad_a = [[0,1],[4,5]], grad_b = [[10,11],[14,15]] — asserts to
1e-12.

Closes frankentorch-w6l2. ft-api lib tests: 1146 passing (+1).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankentorch-vub","title":"Eliminate ft-conformance dead_code warnings blocking clippy","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-27T05:28:12.142290137Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T05:31:52.217395929Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T05:31:52.217160484Z","close_reason":"Validated fixture metadata and removed ft-conformance dead_code warnings","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-vws","title":"Validate performance SLOs from spec against actual measurements","description":"COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENTORCH_V1.md §17 defines 8 performance budgets that have NEVER been measured:

| Path | Workload | Budget |
|---|---|---|
| elementwise forward | 100M elements | p95 <= 200 ms |
| backward pass | representative autograd graphs | p95 <= 1.35x forward baseline |
| matmul core | medium dense matrices | p95 <= 240 ms |
| optimizer step | 10M params Adam | p95 <= 130 ms |
| checkpoint save/load | 2 GB model state | throughput >= 350 MB/s |
| memory footprint | training step profile | peak RSS regression <= +8% |
| allocation churn | backward-heavy trace | alloc count regression <= +10% |
| tail stability | benchmark families | p99 regression <= +7% |

The spec's M4 milestone (Hardening) requires performance gates to pass. Currently we have exactly one benchmark (scalar DAC microbench, p95=4698ns) which doesn't cover any of the 8 SLOs.

Implementation plan:
1. Create a benchmark binary in ft-conformance (or a new crate) that runs each of the 8 workloads
2. For each: run 100 iterations, compute p50/p95/p99, compare against the budget
3. Emit structured JSON results to artifacts/phase2c/performance/
4. Create a gate script that reads the JSON and fails if any SLO is exceeded
5. Document whether FrankenTorch meets, exceeds, or fails each budget

Key consideration: We're CPU-only and pure Rust with no BLAS — we may not meet matmul or elementwise budgets for 100M elements. That's OK — the goal is to MEASURE and know where we stand, then document realistic budgets.

Tests:
- Benchmark binary should be runnable via: rch exec -- cargo run -p ft-conformance --bin run_perf_slos
- JSON output should be machine-readable and diffable
- Gate script should exit 0 (pass) or 1 (fail) with human-readable report","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:17:24.426519762Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:53:59.370070209Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T20:53:59.369819234Z","close_reason":"Implemented perf-SLO runner/gate, validated smoke path, and captured a verified single-sample full-profile measurement with documented direct-budget failures plus baseline-required regression budgets; remaining baseline/CI handoff moved to frankentorch-3cp.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-w4n","title":"Implement F.interpolate (nearest, bilinear, bicubic)","description":"Add tensor_interpolate to ft-api supporting nearest, bilinear, and bicubic modes for 3D/4D/5D tensors. Used by virtually every vision model.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-02T03:25:09.388018146Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T03:29:39.025730530Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T03:29:39.025455069Z","close_reason":"Implemented tensor_interpolate with nearest, linear, bilinear, bicubic, trilinear modes. Supports 3D/4D/5D tensors, size and scale_factor params, align_corners. 10 tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankentorch-w6l2","title":"tensor_block_diag is severed-autograd: linear backward [child of d180/cross/i4rd cluster]","description":"tensor_block_diag at lib.rs:3852 places each input tensor as a block on the diagonal of a larger zero-initialized matrix. PyTorch's torch.block_diag is differentiable. Forward is purely linear in each input — output[row, col] = block_k[r, c] when (row, col) is in block k's region, 0 otherwise.

Backward extracts each block from the corresponding region of grad_output:
   grad_block_k[r, c] = grad_output[row_offset_k + r, col_offset_k + c]

Wrap in tensor_apply_function with a Vec<Option<Vec<f64>>> backward that splits grad_output into per-input blocks. Shapes need to be reconstructed during backward; closure can capture (block_rows, block_cols, total_rows, total_cols) tuples.","notes":"Fixed in this commit. Wrapped tensor_block_diag in tensor_apply_function with per-block extraction backward. Pre-computed (rows, cols, row_offset, col_offset, original_shape) for each input is captured by both the forward and backward closures so the same coordinate-mapping is used in both directions. Backward returns a Vec<Option<Vec<f64>>> with one entry per input, each containing the corresponding sub-region of grad_output. Added block_diag_propagates_gradient_through_each_block regression test (two 2x2 blocks A=[[1,2],[3,4]], B=[[5,6],[7,8]] in a 4x4 output, weighted-sum loss with weights 0..15 → grad_a = [0,1,4,5], grad_b = [10,11,14,15]). ft-api lib tests: 1146.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T18:20:38.093753739Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T18:23:07.389119917Z","closed_at":"2026-04-30T18:23:07.386727212Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-wd1","title":"Add functional batch norm API wrappers in ft-api","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:12:18.738793479Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T17:18:33.991685617Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T17:18:33.991395117Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-wfyq","title":"sign returns -1.0 for -0.0, diverges from torch.sign","description":"sign_scalar and sign_tensor_contiguous_f64 use Rust's f64::signum which returns -1.0 for -0.0 and 1.0 for 0.0 (sign-bit semantics). torch.sign returns 0.0 for both signed zeros. Existing tests at crates/ft-kernel-cpu/src/lib.rs:9188-9206 document the Rust behavior as expected. Fix: replace .signum() with explicit branches that map both signed zeros to 0.0, propagate NaN, and return ±1.0 only for non-zero finite values. Update tests to assert torch parity.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-28T21:23:16.000385332Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-28T21:26:22.951035096Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T21:26:22.949171877Z","close_reason":"sign now matches torch.sign: both signed zeros map to +0.0, NaN propagates as NaN, and ±non-zero return ±1.0. F64 (sign_scalar/sign_tensor_contiguous_f64) and F32 (sign_tensor_contiguous_f32) all routed through new torch_sign_{f32,f64} helpers replacing Rust's IEEE-754-sign-bit signum. 4 new edge-case tests in ft-kernel-cpu + 2 stale ft-autograd tests fixed; ft-kernel-cpu 304 + ft-autograd 314 + ft-api 1086 tests pass; clippy + ubs clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-wq4","title":"Implement tensor_block_diag operation","description":"Add block_diag across the stack. Creates block diagonal matrix from provided tensors. PyTorch signature: torch.block_diag(*tensors). Used in structured ops and GNNs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-27T05:43:19.893738981Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T05:52:26.302493878Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T05:52:26.302253813Z","close_reason":"Implemented tensor_block_diag with 1-D support, 3 tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
